meta.compliance_deadline_basis: 18 months from March 2026 issuance for Deposit Money Banks

executive_summary.lead: Johndup is a standalone Deposit Money Bank operating in a single state with a customer base under 10,000 and daily transaction volumes of 1,000–50,000. The institution faces PEP exposure risk and operates entirely manual AML processes.
body_paragraphs: CBN Circular BSD/DIR/PUB/LAB/019/002 issued March 2026 mandates all Deposit Money Banks deploy integrated AML solutions meeting 12 baseline standards by September 2027. Johndup must submit a detailed Implementation Roadmap by 10 June 2026 — 135 days from now.

This assessment identifies Critical Gaps across all 12 standards. Johndup operates entirely manual AML processes with zero governance controls in place. No MLRO appointed, no board-approved policy, no BVN integration, no internal audit coverage. Governance score: 0/10 (Critical).

Immediate action required. Roadmap submission is non-negotiable. Failure to submit constitutes regulatory non-compliance. OPEX Consulting can complete the CBN template using these findings and guide platform selection within budget constraints identified.
executive_summary.inline_alert: CBN Circular states: 'Institutions without designated MLRO/CCO and board-approved AML policy by roadmap submission date will be subject to immediate supervisory intervention and potential sanctions.'

overall_rating.sector_context_note: DMBs face heightened CBN scrutiny. Manual processes with PEP exposure and zero governance baseline constitute examination-critical deficiencies requiring urgent remediation.

scorecard.regulatory_context_box: DMB classification triggers 18-month compliance timeline ending September 2027. Universal roadmap deadline 10 June 2026 applies regardless of implementation status.

profile.sector_context_box: Deposit Money Banks are Tier 1 supervised entities under CBN prudential framework. Manual AML processes are incompatible with DMB licensing requirements and expose Johndup to immediate supervisory action.

requirement_categories_intro: CBN Circular BSD/DIR/PUB/LAB/019/002 establishes 12 mandatory baseline standards plus governance prerequisites. All requirements apply unconditionally to Deposit Money Banks regardless of size or geographic footprint.
requirement_categories_alert: Johndup must comply with all 17 requirement categories below. Zero conditional exemptions apply to DMB licence holders. Partial compliance is treated as non-compliance for examination purposes.

requirement_categories.area: Integrated AML Solution — core functional footprint
requirement_categories.trigger: Applies to all DMBs unconditionally. Manual processes do not satisfy integrated solution requirement.
DMB retail deposit products require automated CDD. Manual BVN lookups constitute non-compliance.
PEP exposure confirmed. Manual screening against domestic lists only is insufficient for DMB risk profile.
All DMBs must maintain automated risk scoring. Manual assessment does not meet standard.
Trade finance and credit products require real-time monitoring. Manual review is non-compliant.
DMBs must deploy fraud monitoring regardless of channel footprint. ATM/POS operations require coverage.

requirement_categories.area: Case Management — ECM with audit trail
requirement_categories.trigger: Manual case handling does not provide required Maker-Checker controls or immutable audit trail.
goAML portal usage without automation does not satisfy real-time reporting requirement for DMBs.
Manual audit trail does not meet immutability or completeness standards required for DMB examination.
All DMBs must integrate AML solution with core banking. Manual data extraction is non-compliant.

requirement_categories.area: Data Security & Sovereignty — encryption and residency
requirement_categories.trigger: No encryption deployed. Data sovereignty confirmed but security controls absent constitute critical gap.
Manual reporting does not provide board-level dashboards or regulatory analytics required for DMBs.
No board-approved policy in place. Mandatory for all DMBs before roadmap submission.
No MLRO appointed. CBN requires formal designation with board approval before June 2026.
No governance framework documented. Required for all automated AML deployments by DMBs.
Vendor demos seen but no vendor management policy. Mandatory before platform procurement.
Universal deadline 10 June 2026 applies to all institutions. Non-submission triggers supervisory action.

gap_analysis_intro: Johndup exhibits Critical Gaps across all 12 CBN baseline standards. Manual processes, zero governance controls, and absence of foundational policies create immediate examination risk and regulatory non-compliance.

standards.title: Integrated AML Solution
standards.finding: Johndup operates entirely manual AML processes with no integrated platform. Section 5.1 requires automated coverage of CDD, sanctions screening, transaction monitoring, fraud detection, case management, regulatory reporting, risk assessment, and audit trail. Manual processes do not constitute an integrated solution and fail to meet DMB licensing requirements.
standards.required_action: Deploy integrated AML platform covering all 8 core functions before September 2027 compliance deadline. Initiate vendor selection immediately post-roadmap submission.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort provides turnkey integrated AML solution covering all 8 mandatory functions with pre-configured DMB workflows, real-time core banking integration, and CBN-compliant reporting templates.

standards.title: CDD/KYC/KYB
standards.finding: Manual BVN lookups and manual KYC review processes do not satisfy Section 5.2 requirements. DMBs must deploy real-time automated identity verification with BVN/NIN integration, automated UBO mapping for corporate accounts, and dynamic KYC refresh triggers. Manual processes expose Johndup to identity fraud and regulatory sanctions.
standards.required_action: Implement automated CDD with real-time BVN/NIN API integration and automated UBO mapping before compliance deadline. Migrate existing customer base to automated verification.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort includes native BVN/NIN integration via NIBSS APIs, automated UBO discovery for corporate accounts, and risk-based KYC refresh scheduling aligned with CBN tiering requirements.

standards.title: Sanctions & PEP Screening
standards.finding: Manual screening against CBN/EFCC domestic lists only is insufficient. Section 5.3 requires real-time screening against domestic and international sanctions lists including OFAC, UN, EU, and UK HMT. PEP exposure confirmed in risk profile elevates this to examination-critical deficiency. Manual processes cannot detect cross-border PEP relationships or beneficial ownership structures.
standards.required_action: Deploy real-time sanctions and PEP screening covering domestic and international lists with automated adverse media monitoring before compliance deadline.
standards.regtech_solution: RegGuard provides AI-powered real-time screening against 50+ global sanctions lists including OFAC, UN, EU, UK HMT, plus automated PEP relationship mapping and adverse media alerts.

standards.title: Customer Risk Assessment
standards.finding: Manual customer risk assessment does not provide dynamic scoring, automated risk tiering, or event-driven re-assessment required by Section 5.4. DMBs must maintain automated risk models incorporating transaction behaviour, geographic exposure, product usage, and sanctions/PEP hits. Manual assessment cannot scale to regulatory expectations.
standards.required_action: Implement automated customer risk scoring with dynamic re-assessment triggers and board-approved risk appetite thresholds before compliance deadline.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort includes configurable risk scoring engine with 40+ risk factors, automated tiering aligned with CBN categories, and event-driven re-assessment for material changes.

standards.title: Transaction Monitoring
standards.finding: Manual transaction review does not satisfy Section 5.5 requirements for real-time multi-scenario monitoring. Trade finance products require automated detection of structuring, layering, round-tripping, and trade-based money laundering typologies. Manual processes cannot identify complex patterns across customer networks or detect real-time suspicious activity.
standards.required_action: Deploy real-time transaction monitoring with pre-configured trade finance scenarios and network analysis before compliance deadline. Integrate with core banking for real-time data feeds.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort provides 60+ pre-configured monitoring scenarios including trade finance typologies, real-time network analysis, and automated alert generation with risk-based prioritisation.

standards.title: Fraud Monitoring
standards.finding: No fraud monitoring capability deployed. Section 5.6 requires integrated fraud detection with bidirectional feed to customer risk assessment. ATM/POS channel operations expose Johndup to card fraud, account takeover, and synthetic identity fraud. Absence of fraud monitoring creates immediate operational and reputational risk.
standards.required_action: Implement real-time fraud monitoring integrated with AML platform and customer risk engine before compliance deadline. Deploy device fingerprinting and behavioural analytics for ATM/POS channels.
standards.regtech_solution: RegGuard provides real-time fraud detection with machine learning models for card fraud, account takeover, and synthetic identity detection, plus automated risk score updates.

standards.title: Case Management
standards.finding: Manual case handling does not provide Enterprise Case Management with Maker-Checker controls, immutable audit trail, or automated escalation workflows required by Section 5.7. DMBs must maintain full investigation lifecycle management with documented approval chains and regulatory evidence packs. Manual processes cannot demonstrate examination-ready case documentation.
standards.required_action: Deploy ECM platform with Maker-Checker controls, automated escalation, and immutable audit trail before compliance deadline. Migrate open investigations to automated system.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort includes full ECM module with configurable Maker-Checker workflows, automated escalation rules, immutable audit trail, and one-click STR generation from case files.

standards.title: Regulatory Reporting
standards.finding: Manual goAML portal usage with informal approval process does not satisfy Section 5.8 requirements. DMBs must deploy automated STR/CTR generation with documented approval workflows and real-time filing capability. Informal approval processes create regulatory risk and cannot demonstrate compliance with filing deadlines.
standards.required_action: Implement automated regulatory reporting with documented approval workflows and direct goAML integration before compliance deadline. Establish formal MLRO sign-off process.
standards.regtech_solution: RegComply automates STR/CTR generation from case files, enforces documented approval workflows, and provides direct goAML API integration for real-time filing with delivery confirmation.

standards.title: Audit Trail
standards.finding: Manual audit trail does not provide immutable logging, tamper-proof evidence, or comprehensive activity tracking required by Section 5.9. AML not covered by internal audit constitutes dual critical gap. DMBs must maintain complete audit trail of all AML activities with internal audit review at least twice yearly.
standards.required_action: Deploy immutable audit trail capability and establish internal audit coverage of AML processes before compliance deadline. Conduct initial AML audit within 90 days of platform deployment.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort provides blockchain-anchored immutable audit trail capturing all system activities, user actions, and configuration changes with tamper-proof evidence for examination purposes.

standards.title: Core Banking Integration
standards.finding: No automated core banking integration exists. Section 5.10 requires bidirectional real-time integration for customer data, transaction feeds, and risk score updates. Manual data extraction creates latency, data quality issues, and prevents real-time monitoring required for DMB operations.
standards.required_action: Establish bidirectional real-time integration between AML platform and core banking system before compliance deadline. Implement automated data validation and reconciliation controls.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort includes pre-built connectors for major core banking platforms used in Nigeria with bidirectional real-time APIs, automated data validation, and reconciliation dashboards.

standards.title: Data Security & Sovereignty
standards.finding: No encryption deployed and MFA not enforced constitute critical security gaps. Section 5.11 requires encryption at rest and in transit, MFA for all users, and Nigeria data residency. Data sovereignty confirmed but absence of encryption and MFA creates immediate data breach risk and regulatory non-compliance.
standards.required_action: Implement full encryption at rest and in transit, enforce MFA for all AML system users, and conduct security audit before compliance deadline.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort deploys with AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, mandatory MFA, and Nigeria-based cloud infrastructure meeting CBN data sovereignty requirements.

standards.title: Reporting & Analytics
standards.finding: Manual reporting does not provide real-time dashboards, board-level analytics, or regulatory reporting templates required by Section 5.12. DMBs must maintain automated dashboards for MLRO, board, and CBN examination purposes with drill-down capability and trend analysis.
standards.required_action: Deploy automated reporting and analytics platform with board dashboards and regulatory templates before compliance deadline. Establish monthly board reporting cadence.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort includes pre-configured dashboards for MLRO, board, and regulators with real-time KPIs, trend analysis, and one-click regulatory report generation.

governance_assessment.intro: Johndup has zero governance controls in place. No MLRO appointed, no board-approved policy, no documented frameworks. This constitutes examination-critical deficiency requiring immediate remediation before roadmap submission.
governance_assessment.score_context: 0/10 governance controls in place represents critical non-compliance. CBN requires foundational governance baseline before platform deployment. Absence of MLRO and board policy alone triggers supervisory intervention risk.

items.action_required: Appoint MLRO with board approval before roadmap submission deadline.
Draft and obtain board approval for comprehensive AML policy before June 2026.
Develop governance framework covering roles, escalation, and oversight before platform deployment.

items.control: Formal Change Control for AML Configurations
items.action_required: Establish change control process with Maker-Checker approval before platform go-live.
Not immediately required. Establish if AI/ML capabilities deployed in future.
Document alert review SLAs aligned with risk tiering before platform deployment.
Adopt vendor management policy before platform procurement and contracting.
Document data retention policy aligned with CBN 7-year requirement before platform deployment.
Establish real-time BVN/NIN integration via NIBSS APIs before compliance deadline.

items.control: AML Training Programme with Documented Records
items.action_required: Deploy AML training programme with attendance tracking before compliance deadline.

priority_actions.title: CBN Implementation Roadmap Submission
priority_actions.deadline_label: HARD DEADLINE: 10 JUNE 2026 · MANDATORY FOR ALL INSTITUTIONS
priority_actions.body:
Johndup must submit the CBN Implementation Roadmap Template by 10 June 2026. This is a universal non-negotiable deadline applying to all financial institutions regardless of implementation status. The roadmap must cover 12 sections including executive summary, gap analysis, implementation strategy, timeline, resource plan, governance framework, and board sign-off. Incomplete or missing submissions constitute regulatory non-compliance and trigger immediate supervisory action.

OPEX Consulting can complete the CBN template using findings from this assessment. We provide turnkey roadmap preparation including gap analysis documentation, phased implementation timeline, resource planning, and board presentation materials. Roadmap completion typically requires 2-3 weeks from engagement to final board approval.

priority_actions.title: MLRO/CCO Appointment
priority_actions.body:
CBN Circular requires formal MLRO or CCO designation with board approval before roadmap submission. The MLRO must be a senior officer with direct board reporting line, authority to halt suspicious transactions, and independence from business units. Job description must specify AML/CFT responsibilities, qualifications, and reporting obligations.

OPEX Consulting provides interim MLRO services during recruitment and can draft job descriptions, board resolutions, and MLRO terms of reference aligned with CBN requirements. We also offer MLRO training and certification preparation.

priority_actions.title: Board-Approved AML/CFT/CPF Policy
priority_actions.body:
Johndup must obtain board approval for comprehensive AML/CFT/CPF policy before June 2026 roadmap deadline. The policy must cover customer due diligence, sanctions screening, transaction monitoring, suspicious activity reporting, record retention, training, and governance. Policy must be tailored to Johndup's DMB licence, product profile, and risk factors.

OPEX Consulting drafts CBN-compliant AML policies customised to institution type and risk profile. We provide policy templates, board presentation materials, and implementation guidance. Policy development typically requires 3-4 weeks from kickoff to board approval.

priority_actions.title: AML Platform Selection and Procurement
priority_actions.body:
Johndup has seen vendor demos but requires formal platform selection aligned with budget constraints. Platform must cover all 8 core functions required by Section 5.1 with real-time core banking integration, BVN/NIN APIs, and Nigeria data residency. Procurement must follow documented vendor management process.

OPEX Consulting provides vendor evaluation support including RFP preparation, demo facilitation, scoring matrix development, and contract negotiation. We maintain vendor-neutral advisory stance and can guide RegTech365 product evaluation alongside alternatives. Budget-conscious institutions benefit from our procurement optimisation and phased deployment strategies.

priority_actions.title: Internal Audit Coverage of AML
priority_actions.body:
AML currently not covered by internal audit. CBN requires internal audit review of AML processes at least twice yearly with documented findings and remediation tracking. First audit should occur within 90 days of platform deployment to validate configuration, test scenarios, and verify governance controls.

OPEX Consulting offers internal audit co-sourcing for AML/CFT including audit planning, fieldwork execution, findings documentation, and remediation tracking. We provide CBN-aligned audit programmes and can train internal audit teams on AML examination techniques.

roadmap.intro: This roadmap guides Johndup from current manual processes to full CBN compliance by September 2027. Four phases span 18 months covering governance foundation, platform deployment, integration, and examination readiness.

phases.title: Foundation & Roadmap Submission
phases.description: Establish governance baseline and submit CBN roadmap. Appoint MLRO with board approval. Draft and approve AML/CFT/CPF policy. Document vendor management policy. Complete CBN Implementation Roadmap Template covering all 12 sections. Obtain board sign-off and submit to CBN Compliance Department by 10 June 2026 deadline.

phases.title: Governance & Architecture
phases.description: Complete governance framework and design technical architecture. Document AML Solution Governance Framework covering roles, escalation, and oversight. Establish change control process with Maker-Checker approval. Design core banking integration architecture. Develop data retention policy. Document alert review SLAs. Conduct vendor selection and contract negotiation.

phases.title: Platform Deployment & Integration
phases.description: Deploy integrated AML platform and establish core integrations. Implement RegPort covering CDD, sanctions screening, transaction monitoring, case management, and regulatory reporting. Deploy RegGuard for fraud monitoring. Establish real-time BVN/NIN integration via NIBSS APIs. Configure bidirectional core banking integration. Implement encryption and MFA. Migrate existing customer base to automated CDD. Configure monitoring scenarios for trade finance and retail banking. Conduct user acceptance testing.
deliverables: Customer base migrated to automated CDD

phases.title: Assurance & Examination Readiness
phases.description: Validate compliance and prepare for CBN examination. Conduct internal audit of AML processes with documented findings. Deploy AML training programme via RegLearn with attendance tracking. Prepare examination evidence pack covering all 12 standards. Conduct board AML briefing with compliance dashboard review. Validate immutable audit trail and regulatory reporting workflows. Conduct dry-run CBN examination simulation. Remediate any findings before compliance deadline.

milestones.milestone: MLRO appointed and board-approved AML policy in place
Vendor contract executed and governance framework documented
Platform deployment complete with core banking integration live
Internal audit completed and training programme deployed
Full CBN compliance achieved and examination-ready

support_section.intro_paragraph: OPEX Consulting and RegTech365 provide end-to-end support for Johndup's AML transformation. We combine regulatory advisory, platform deployment, and ongoing assurance to deliver turnkey CBN compliance within budget constraints.
support_section.differentiator: OPEX Consulting is the only Nigerian advisory firm offering integrated RegTech365 deployment with CBN roadmap preparation, policy drafting, and internal audit co-sourcing. We understand DMB budget realities and structure phased implementations aligned with cash flow.

products.name: RegPort
products.tagline: Integrated AML Platform for CBN Baseline Standards Compliance
products.description: RegPort unifies CDD/KYC, sanctions/PEP screening, transaction monitoring, customer risk assessment, and regulatory reporting in a single platform. The platform is purpose-built for Nigerian financial institutions, featuring real-time BVN/NIN integration via NIBSS, automated goAML/NFIU filing, and AI-enabled fuzzy matching for international sanctions lists.

products.name: RegGuard
products.tagline: Real-Time Fraud Monitoring with Bidirectional Risk Feed
products.description: RegGuard provides real-time fraud monitoring for card issuance, mobile money, and agent network channels. It detects pattern-based fraud like SIM swap attacks and account takeover, with an automated feed that pushes fraud alerts directly into customer risk scores to identify money laundering disguised as fraud.

products.name: RegComply
products.tagline: Enterprise Case Management with Immutable Audit Trails
products.description: RegComply delivers the mandatory Case Management and Governance Audit layer. It enforces Maker-Checker workflows for investigation, provides role-based access for MLROs, and maintains tamper-proof audit logs of all configuration changes and alert dispositions required for CBN examination.

products.name: RegLearn
products.tagline: AML Training Programme with Documented Records
products.description: RegLearn provides role-specific AML/CFT/CPF training modules covering the 12 Baseline Standards. The platform tracks staff completion, assessment scores, and certification status, producing the documented training records specifically mandated under Section 6.7 of the Circular.
